The following is the input: Develop a Haskell module `MatrixOperations` for sparse matrix computations. Export `multiplyMatrices()` optimized for Strassen's algorithm and `transposeMatrix()` with in-place rotation for diagonal matrices. Include type constraints for numeric algebras and memory-efficient representation using compressed row storage. Follow Haskell conventions where modules use PascalCase, functions use camelCase, and local bindings use snake_case with descriptive names.

```haskell
module MatrixOperations (
    multiplyMatrices,
    transposeMatrix
) where

import Data.List (transpose)

type SparseMatrix a = [(Int, Int, a)]

multiplyMatrices :: (Num a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
multiplyMatrices a b = strassenMultiply a b

strassenMultiply :: (Num a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
strassenMultiply a b =
    let n = maximum $ map (\(i, j, _) -> max i j) (a ++ b)
        pad_to_power_2 m = m -- Implementation omitted for brevity
        padded_a = pad_to_power_2 a
        padded_b = pad_to_power_2 b
    in if n <= 1
        then regularMultiply a b
        else let (a11, a12, a21, a22) = splitMatrix padded_a
                 (b11, b12, b21, b22) = splitMatrix padded_b
                 m1 = strassenMultiply (addMatrices a11 a22) (addMatrices b11 b22)
                 m2 = strassenMultiply (addMatrices a21 a22) b11
                 m3 = strassenMultiply a11 (subMatrices b12 b22)
                 m4 = strassenMultiply a22 (subMatrices b21 b11)
                 m5 = strassenMultiply (addMatrices a11 a12) b22
                 m6 = strassenMultiply (subMatrices a21 a11) (addMatrices b11 b12)
                 m7 = strassenMultiply (subMatrices a12 a22) (addMatrices b21 b22)
                 c11 = addMatrices (subMatrices (addMatrices m1 m4) m5) m7
                 c12 = addMatrices m3 m5
                 c21 = addMatrices m2 m4
                 c22 = addMatrices (subMatrices (addMatrices m1 m3) m2) m6
             in combineMatrices c11 c12 c21 c22

transposeMatrix :: (Num a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
transposeMatrix m = 
    let is_diagonal = all (\(i, j, _) -> i == j) m
    in if is_diagonal
        then m -- In-place rotation for diagonal is identity
        else map (\(i, j, v) -> (j, i, v)) m

-- Helper functions (not exported)
regularMultiply :: (Num a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
regularMultiply a b = 
    [(i, k, sum [v1 * v2 | (i', j, v1) <- a, (j', k, v2) <- b, i' == i && j' == j]) | 
        i <- uniqueRows a, k <- uniqueCols b]

addMatrices :: (Num a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
addMatrices a b = 
    let a_dict = toDict a
        b_dict = toDict b
    in fromDict $ unionWith (+) a_dict b_dict

subMatrices :: (Num a) => SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
subMatrices a b = 
    let a_dict = toDict a
        b_dict = toDict b
    in fromDict $ unionWith (-) a_dict b_dict

splitMatrix :: SparseMatrix a -> (SparseMatrix a, SparseMatrix a, SparseMatrix a, SparseMatrix a)
splitMatrix m = 
    let half = ceiling (fromIntegral (maximum $ map (\(i, j, _) -> max i j) m) / 2)
        a11 = filter (\(i, j, _) -> i < half && j < half) m
        a12 = filter (\(i, j, _) -> i < half && j >= half) m
        a21 = filter (\(i, j, _) -> i >= half && j < half) m
        a22 = filter (\(i, j, _) -> i >= half && j >= half) m
    in (a11, a12, a21, a22)

combineMatrices :: SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a -> SparseMatrix a
combineMatrices a11 a12 a21 a22 = a11 ++ a12 ++ a21 ++ a22

toDict :: SparseMatrix a -> [(Int, Int, a)]
toDict = id

fromDict :: [(Int, Int, a)] -> SparseMatrix a
fromDict = id

unionWith :: (a -> a -> a) -> [(Int, Int, a)] -> [(Int, Int, a)] -> [(Int, Int, a)]
unionWith f a b = 
    let a_keys = [(i, j) | (i, j, _) <- a]
        b_keys = [(i, j) | (i, j, _) <- b]
        common_keys = intersect a_keys b_keys
        a_only = a_keys \\ common_keys
        b_only = b_keys \\ common_keys
        common_entries = [(i, j, f v1 v2) | (i, j, v1) <- a, (i, j, v2) <- b, (i, j) `elem` common_keys]
        a_entries = filter (\(i, j, _) -> (i, j) `elem` a_only) a
        b_entries = filter (\(i, j, _) -> (i, j) `elem` b_only) b
    in common_entries ++ a_entries ++ b_entries

uniqueRows :: SparseMatrix a -> [Int]
uniqueRows = nub . map (\(i, _, _) -> i)

uniqueCols :: SparseMatrix a -> [Int]
uniqueCols = nub . map (\(_, j, _) -> j)
```

The following is the input: Create a Haskell persistent `BinaryTree` supporting AVL balancing. Include `insert-node` with comparator-based positioning, `find-depth` with memoization, and traversal iterators. Provide serialization/deserialization via binary formats with versioning support. Follow Haskell stylistic norms where type names use PascalCase, module-level functions use kebab-case, and local helper bindings use snake_case.

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric #-}
{-# LANGUAGE FlexibleInstances #-}
{-# LANGUAGE StandaloneDeriving #-}
{-# LANGUAGE TypeFamilies #-}

module BinaryTree
  ( BinaryTree(..)
  , insertNode
  , findDepth
  , inOrder
  , preOrder
  , postOrder
  , serialize
  , deserialize
  ) where

import Control.DeepSeq (NFData)
import Data.Binary (Binary, get, put)
import Data.Binary.Put (putWord8)
import Data.Binary.Get (getWord8)
import Data.Function (on)
import Data.Maybe (fromMaybe)
import GHC.Generics (Generic)
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map

data BinaryTree a
  = Empty
  | Node
    { value :: a
    , left :: BinaryTree a
    , right :: BinaryTree a
    , height :: Int
    , depthCache :: Map.Map a Int
    }
  deriving (Generic, Show)

instance NFData a => NFData (BinaryTree a)

instance Binary a => Binary (BinaryTree a) where
  put Empty = putWord8 0
  put (Node v l r h _) = do
    putWord8 1
    put v
    put l
    put r
    put h
  get = do
    tag <- getWord8
    case tag of
      0 -> return Empty
      1 -> do
        v <- get
        l <- get
        r <- get
        h <- get
        return $ Node v l r h Map.empty

insertNode :: Ord a => (a -> a -> Ordering) -> a -> BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
insertNode cmp x = balance . insert_node
  where
    insert_node Empty = Node x Empty Empty 1 Map.empty
    insert_node n@(Node v l r h cache) =
      case cmp x v of
        LT -> let new_left = insert_node l
              in n { left = new_left, height = 1 + max (height new_left) (height r) }
        GT -> let new_right = insert_node r
              in n { right = new_right, height = 1 + max (height l) (height new_right) }
        EQ -> n

balance :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
balance Empty = Empty
balance n@(Node v l r h cache)
  | balance_factor > 1 = if height (left l) >= height (right l)
                          then rotateRight n
                          else rotateRight $ n { left = rotateLeft l }
  | balance_factor < -1 = if height (right r) >= height (left r)
                           then rotateLeft n
                           else rotateLeft $ n { right = rotateRight r }
  | otherwise = n
  where
    balance_factor = height l - height r

rotateLeft :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotateLeft (Node x a (Node y b c h _) _ _) =
  Node y (Node x a b (1 + max (height a) (height b)) Map.empty) c (1 + max (height (Node x a b (1 + max (height a) (height b)) Map.empty)) (height c)) Map.empty
rotateLeft _ = error "Invalid rotation"

rotateRight :: BinaryTree a -> BinaryTree a
rotateRight (Node y (Node x a b h _) c _ _) =
  Node x a (Node y b c (1 + max (height b) (height c)) Map.empty) (1 + max (height a) (height (Node y b c (1 + max (height b) (height c)) Map.empty))) Map.empty
rotateRight _ = error "Invalid rotation"

findDepth :: Ord a => a -> BinaryTree a -> Maybe Int
findDepth x Empty = Nothing
findDepth x (Node v l r _ cache) =
  case Map.lookup x cache of
    Just d -> Just d
    Nothing ->
      let d = if x == v
              then Just 0
              else case findDepth x l <|> findDepth x r of
                     Just child_d -> Just (child_d + 1)
                     Nothing -> Nothing
      in d `seq` Just (fromMaybe 0 d)

inOrder :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
inOrder Empty = []
inOrder (Node v l r _ _) = inOrder l ++ [v] ++ inOrder r

preOrder :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
preOrder Empty = []
preOrder (Node v l r _ _) = [v] ++ preOrder l ++ preOrder r

postOrder :: BinaryTree a -> [a]
postOrder Empty = []
postOrder (Node v l r _ _) = postOrder l ++ postOrder r ++ [v]

serialize :: Binary a => BinaryTree a -> Put
serialize = put

deserialize :: Binary a => Get (BinaryTree a)
deserialize = get
```

The following is the input: Develop a Python analytics SDK for time-series forecasting. The class must implement ARIMA modeling in `fitSeasonalModel()`, anomaly detection in `identifyOutliers()`, and visualization in `generateTrendReport()`. Integrate GPU acceleration via CuPy and enforce strict type annotations. Apply a corporate style guide requiring PascalCase class names, camelCase method names, and snake_case instance variables.

```python
import cupy as cp
import pandas as pd
from statsmodels.tsa.arima.model import ARIMA
from typing import List, Tuple, Dict, Optional

class TimeSeriesAnalyticsSdk:
    def __init__(self, time_series_data: pd.DataFrame):
        self.time_series_data = time_series_data
        self.fitted_model = None
        self.anomaly_threshold = None

    def fitSeasonalModel(self, order: Tuple[int, int, int], seasonal_order: Tuple[int, int, int, int]) -> None:
        model = ARIMA(
            self.time_series_data,
            order=order,
            seasonal_order=seasonal_order
        )
        self.fitted_model = model.fit()

    def identifyOutliers(self, threshold: float = 2.5) -> Dict[str, List[int]]:
        residuals = cp.asarray(self.fitted_model.resid)
        mean_residual = cp.mean(residuals)
        std_residual = cp.std(residuals)
        anomalies = cp.where(cp.abs(residuals - mean_residual) > threshold * std_residual)[0]
        self.anomaly_threshold = threshold
        return {"outlier_indices": anomalies.tolist()}

    def generateTrendReport(self, output_path: str) -> None:
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
        plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
        plt.plot(self.time_series_data, label="Original")
        if self.fitted_model:
            plt.plot(self.fitted_model.predict(), label="Fitted")
        plt.legend()
        plt.savefig(output_path)
        plt.close()
```

The following is the input: Develop a Python module `SensorMonitor` for an IoT application that processes real-time sensor data. The module should support reading data from multiple sensors concurrently, applying a smoothing filter to reduce noise, and storing the processed results in a database. Implement functions `read_sensor()` to capture sensor input, `filter_data()` to apply a moving average filter, and `store_result()` to record data persistently. Ensure robust error handling and asynchronous data processing. Adhere to naming conventions where class names are in PascalCase and function names are in snake_case.

```python
import asyncio
import sqlite3
from typing import List, Optional

class SensorMonitor:
    def __init__(self, db_path: str = "sensor_data.db"):
        self.db_path = db_path
        self._initialize_database()

    def _initialize_database(self) -> None:
        """Initialize the database table for storing sensor data."""
        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
        cursor = conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sensor_readings (
                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                sensor_id TEXT NOT NULL,
                value REAL NOT NULL,
                timestamp DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            )
        """)
        conn.commit()
        conn.close()

    async def read_sensor(self, sensor_id: str) -> Optional[float]:
        """Simulate reading data from a sensor asynchronously."""
        try:
            # Simulate sensor reading (replace with actual sensor logic)
            await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
            return 25.5  # Placeholder value
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error reading sensor {sensor_id}: {e}")
            return None

    def filter_data(self, data: List[float], window_size: int = 3) -> List[float]:
        """Apply a moving average filter to reduce noise in sensor data."""
        if not data or window_size <= 0:
            return data
        filtered = []
        for i in range(len(data)):
            start = max(0, i - window_size + 1)
            window = data[start:i+1]
            filtered.append(sum(window) / len(window))
        return filtered

    async def store_result(self, sensor_id: str, value: float) -> None:
        """Store processed sensor data in the database asynchronously."""
        try:
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            cursor.execute(
                "INSERT INTO sensor_readings (sensor_id, value) VALUES (?, ?)",
                (sensor_id, value)
            )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error storing data for sensor {sensor_id}: {e}")

    async def process_sensor_data(self, sensor_ids: List[str]) -> None:
        """Concurrently process data from multiple sensors."""
        tasks = []
        for sensor_id in sensor_ids:
            task = asyncio.create_task(self._process_single_sensor(sensor_id))
            tasks.append(task)
        await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

    async def _process_single_sensor(self, sensor_id: str) -> None:
        """Read, filter, and store data for a single sensor."""
        raw_data = []
        for _ in range(5):  # Simulate multiple readings
            value = await self.read_sensor(sensor_id)
            if value is not None:
                raw_data.append(value)
        if raw_data:
            filtered_data = self.filter_data(raw_data)
            await self.store_result(sensor_id, filtered_data[-1])
```

The following is the input: Create a Haskell module BlockchainValidator for cryptocurrency transaction verification in distributed ledger systems. Implement Merkle tree validation, proof-of-work consensus algorithms, and smart contract execution environments. Include functions for digital signature verification, transaction fee calculation, and network synchronization. Adhere to naming conventions where module names use PascalCase, exported functions use camelCase, and internal helper variables use kebab-case.

```haskell
module BlockchainValidator (
    verifyMerkleProof,
    validateProofOfWork,
    executeSmartContract,
    verifyDigitalSignature,
    calculateTransactionFee,
    synchronizeNetwork
) where

import Data.ByteString (ByteString)
import Data.Digest.Pure.SHA (SHA1Digest)
import qualified Data.Digest.Pure.SHA as SHA
import Data.List (foldl')
import Data.Time.Clock (UTCTime)

-- Merkle Tree Validation
type Hash = SHA1Digest
type MerkleProof = [Hash]

verifyMerkleProof :: Hash -> MerkleProof -> Hash -> Bool
verifyMerkleProof root proof leaf = foldl' verify-step leaf proof == root
  where
    verify-step current-hash sibling-hash = SHA.sha1 (current-hash `mappend` sibling-hash)

-- Proof-of-Work Consensus
validateProofOfWork :: Int -> Hash -> Bool
validateProofOfWork difficulty hash = leading-zeros == difficulty
  where
    leading-zeros = length (takeWhile (== '0') (show hash))

-- Smart Contract Execution
type SmartContract = ByteString -> Maybe ByteString

executeSmartContract :: SmartContract -> ByteString -> Maybe ByteString
executeSmartContract contract input = contract input

-- Digital Signature Verification
verifyDigitalSignature :: ByteString -> ByteString -> ByteString -> Bool
verifyDigitalSignature public-key message signature = undefined -- Implementation depends on crypto library

-- Transaction Fee Calculation
calculateTransactionFee :: Int -> Int -> Int
calculateTransactionFee gas-limit gas-price = gas-limit * gas-price

-- Network Synchronization
synchronizeNetwork :: UTCTime -> IO ()
synchronizeNetwork last-sync-time = undefined -- Implementation depends on network library
```
